text: Confucius insisted that the superior person must examine the quality of their attention as well as its object: "When you know a thing, to hold that you know it; and when you do not know a thing, to allow that you do not know it — this is knowledge." This epistemic discipline — refusing false certainty — is identical to what psychologist David Dunning calls metacognitive accuracy. Overconfident learners stop paying attention because they believe they have already mastered material; accurate self-assessment keeps the mind open and searching. For Confucius, intellectual humility was not modesty but precision: the condition that allows learning to continue.
question: According to the passage, why does Confucius value admitting what you do not know?

options.text: It demonstrates deference to more experienced teachers
It maintains the open, searching attention that allows learning to continue
It prevents social embarrassment in scholarly debate
It fulfills the ritual requirement of humility before elders
